function.name: page_length
function.description: Render a structured resume JSON via the configured LaTeX template, compile it, and return {ok, fill, page_cap, short_bullets}. `fill` is a decimal: 1.0 == exactly one page; 1.25 == 25% into a second page; 0.8 == 80% of one page. `short_bullets` is a list of bullets whose final wrapped line is mostly empty (a few words spilling onto their own line) — wasted vertical space the writer should either tighten so the bullet fits on one fewer line, or strengthen so the wrap is justified. Each entry is {text, lines, last_line_fill} where `last_line_fill` is the fraction (0–1) of the line width the trailing wrap consumes. Use this to decide whether to add, tighten, or drop content.

resume.description: The resume JSON matching generate_resume.schema.json — a single top-level `sections` array. Do not include header/contact info; the server injects it before rendering.
